root_text.an3.64:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati gijjhakūṭe pabbate.
root_text.an3.64:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena sarabho nāma paribbājako acirapakkanto hoti imasmā dhammavinayā.
root_text.an3.64:1.4: So rājagahe parisati evaṁ vācaṁ bhāsati:
root_text.an3.64:1.6: Aññāya ca panāhaṁ samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtikānaṁ dhammaṁ evāhaṁ tasmā dhammavinayā apakkanto”ti.
root_text.an3.64:1.7: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya rājagahaṁ piṇḍāya pavisiṁsu.
root_text.an3.64:1.8: Assosuṁ kho te bhikkhū sarabhassa paribbājakassa rājagahe parisati evaṁ vācaṁ bhāsamānassa:
root_text.an3.64:1.10: Aññāya ca panāhaṁ samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtikānaṁ dhammaṁ evāhaṁ tasmā dhammavinayā apakkanto”ti.
root_text.an3.64:2.1: Atha kho te bhikkhū rājagahe piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an3.64:2.2: “sarabho nāma, bhante, paribbājako acirapakkanto imasmā dhammavinayā.
root_text.an3.64:2.3: So rājagahe parisati evaṁ vācaṁ bhāsati:
root_text.an3.64:2.5: Aññāya ca panāhaṁ samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtikānaṁ dhammaṁ evāhaṁ tasmā dhammavinayā apakkanto’ti.
root_text.an3.64:2.6: Sādhu, bhante, bhagavā yena sippinikātīraṁ paribbājakārāmo yena sarabho paribbājako tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā”ti.
root_text.an3.64:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā sāyanhasamayaṁ paṭisallānā vuṭṭhito yena sippinikātīraṁ paribbājakārāmo yena sarabho paribbājako tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. Nisajja kho bhagavā sarabhaṁ paribbājakaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.64:3.4: Aññāya ca panāhaṁ samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtikānaṁ dhammaṁ evāhaṁ tasmā dhammavinayā apakkanto’”ti?
root_text.an3.64:3.5: Evaṁ vutte, sarabho paribbājako tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.an3.64:4.1: Dutiyampi kho bhagavā sarabhaṁ paribbājakaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.64:4.2: “vadehi, sarabha, kinti te aññāto samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtikānaṁ dhammo?
root_text.an3.64:4.3: Sace te aparipūraṁ bhavissati, ahaṁ paripūressāmi.
root_text.an3.64:4.4: Sace pana te paripūraṁ bhavissati, ahaṁ anumodissāmī”ti.
root_text.an3.64:4.5: Dutiyampi kho sarabho paribbājako tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.an3.64:5.1: Tatiyampi kho bhagavā sarabhaṁ paribbājakaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an3.64:5.2: “yo kho, sarabha, paññāyati samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtikānaṁ dhammo vadehi, sarabha, kinti te aññāto samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtikānaṁ dhammo?
root_text.an3.64:5.3: Sace te aparipūraṁ bhavissati, ahaṁ paripūressāmi.
root_text.an3.64:5.4: Sace pana te paripūraṁ bhavissati, ahaṁ anumodissāmī”ti.
root_text.an3.64:5.5: Tatiyampi kho sarabho paribbājako tuṇhī ahosi.
root_text.an3.64:6.1: Atha kho te paribbājakā sarabhaṁ paribbājakaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an3.64:6.2: “yadeva kho tvaṁ, āvuso sarabha, samaṇaṁ gotamaṁ yāceyyāsi tadeva te samaṇo gotamo pavāreti.
root_text.an3.64:6.3: Vadehāvuso sarabha, kinti te aññāto samaṇānaṁ sakyaputtikānaṁ dhammo?
root_text.an3.64:6.4: Sace te aparipūraṁ bhavissati, samaṇo gotamo paripūressati.
root_text.an3.64:6.5: Sace pana te paripūraṁ bhavissati, samaṇo gotamo anumodissatī”ti.
root_text.an3.64:6.6: Evaṁ vutte, sarabho paribbājako tuṇhībhūto maṅkubhūto pattakkhandho adhomukho pajjhāyanto appaṭibhāno nisīdi.
root_text.an3.64:7.1: Atha kho bhagavā sarabhaṁ paribbājakaṁ tuṇhībhūtaṁ maṅkubhūtaṁ pattakkhandhaṁ adhomukhaṁ pajjhāyantaṁ appaṭibhānaṁ viditvā te paribbājake etadavoca:
root_text.an3.64:8.2: ‘sammāsambuddhassa te paṭijānato ime dhammā anabhisambuddhā’ti, tamahaṁ tattha sādhukaṁ samanuyuñjeyyaṁ samanugāheyyaṁ samanubhāseyyaṁ.
root_text.an3.64:8.3: So vata mayā sādhukaṁ samanuyuñjiyamāno samanugāhiyamāno samanubhāsiyamāno aṭṭhānametaṁ anavakāso yaṁ so tiṇṇaṁ ṭhānānaṁ nāññataraṁ ṭhānaṁ nigaccheyya, aññena vā aññaṁ paṭicarissati, bahiddhā kathaṁ apanāmessati, kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukarissati, tuṇhībhūto maṅkubhūto pattakkhandho adhomukho pajjhāyanto appaṭibhāno nisīdissati, seyyathāpi sarabho paribbājako.
root_text.an3.64:9.1: Yo kho maṁ, paribbājakā, evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an3.64:9.2: ‘khīṇāsavassa te paṭijānato ime āsavā aparikkhīṇā’ti, tamahaṁ tattha sādhukaṁ samanuyuñjeyyaṁ samanugāheyyaṁ samanubhāseyyaṁ.
root_text.an3.64:9.3: So vata mayā sādhukaṁ samanuyuñjiyamāno samanugāhiyamāno samanubhāsiyamāno aṭṭhānametaṁ anavakāso yaṁ so tiṇṇaṁ ṭhānānaṁ nāññataraṁ ṭhānaṁ nigaccheyya, aññena vā aññaṁ paṭicarissati, bahiddhā kathaṁ apanāmessati, kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukarissati, tuṇhībhūto maṅkubhūto pattakkhandho adhomukho pajjhāyanto appaṭibhāno nisīdissati, seyyathāpi sarabho paribbājako.
root_text.an3.64:10.1: Yo kho maṁ, paribbājakā, evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an3.64:10.2: ‘yassa kho pana te atthāya dhammo desito, so na niyyāti takkarassa sammā dukkhakkhayāyā’ti, tamahaṁ tattha sādhukaṁ samanuyuñjeyyaṁ samanugāheyyaṁ samanubhāseyyaṁ.
root_text.an3.64:10.3: So vata mayā sādhukaṁ samanuyuñjiyamāno samanugāhiyamāno samanubhāsiyamāno aṭṭhānametaṁ anavakāso yaṁ so tiṇṇaṁ ṭhānānaṁ nāññataraṁ ṭhānaṁ nigaccheyya, aññena vā aññaṁ paṭicarissati, bahiddhā kathaṁ apanāmessati, kopañca dosañca appaccayañca pātukarissati, tuṇhībhūto maṅkubhūto pattakkhandho adhomukho pajjhāyanto appaṭibhāno nisīdissati, seyyathāpi sarabho paribbājako”ti.
root_text.an3.64:10.4: Atha kho bhagavā sippinikātīre paribbājakārāme tikkhattuṁ sīhanādaṁ naditvā vehāsaṁ pakkāmi.
root_text.an3.64:11.1: Atha kho te paribbājakā acirapakkantassa bhagavato sarabhaṁ paribbājakaṁ samantato vācāyasannitodakena sañjambharimakaṁsu:
root_text.an3.64:11.2: “seyyathāpi, āvuso sarabha, brahāraññe jarasiṅgālo ‘sīhanādaṁ nadissāmī’ti siṅgālakaṁyeva nadati, bheraṇḍakaṁyeva nadati;
root_text.an3.64:11.3: evamevaṁ kho tvaṁ, āvuso sarabha, aññatreva samaṇena gotamena ‘sīhanādaṁ nadissāmī’ti siṅgālakaṁyeva nadasi bheraṇḍakaṁyeva nadasi.
root_text.an3.64:11.4: Seyyathāpi, āvuso sarabha, ambukasañcarī ‘purisakaravitaṁ ravissāmī’ti ambukasañcariravitaṁyeva ravati;
root_text.an3.64:11.5: evamevaṁ kho tvaṁ, āvuso sarabha, aññatreva samaṇena gotamena ‘purisakaravitaṁ ravissāmī’ti, ambukasañcariravitaṁyeva ravasi.
root_text.an3.64:11.6: Seyyathāpi, āvuso sarabha, usabho suññāya gosālāya gambhīraṁ naditabbaṁ maññati;
root_text.an3.64:11.7: evamevaṁ kho tvaṁ, āvuso sarabha, aññatreva samaṇena gotamena gambhīraṁ naditabbaṁ maññasī”ti.
root_text.an3.64:11.8: Atha kho te paribbājakā sarabhaṁ paribbājakaṁ samantato vācāyasannitodakena sañjambharimakaṁsūti.

translation_text.an3.64:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, on the Vulture’s Peak Mountain.
translation_text.an3.64:1.3: Now at that time a wanderer named Sarabha had recently left this teaching and training.
translation_text.an3.64:1.4: He was telling a crowd in Rājagaha,
translation_text.an3.64:1.5: “I learned the teaching of the ascetics who follow the Sakyan,
translation_text.an3.64:1.6: then I left their teaching and training.”
translation_text.an3.64:1.7: Then several mendicants robed up in the morning and, taking their bowls and robes, entered Rājagaha for alms.
translation_text.an3.64:1.8: They heard what Sarabha was saying.
translation_text.an3.64:2.1: Then, after the meal, when they returned from almsround, they went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an3.64:2.2: “The wanderer named Sarabha has recently left this teaching and training.
translation_text.an3.64:2.3: He was telling a crowd in Rājagaha:
translation_text.an3.64:2.4: ‘I learned the teaching of the ascetics who follow the Sakyan,
translation_text.an3.64:2.5: then I left their teaching and training.’
translation_text.an3.64:2.6: Worthy sir, please go to the wanderers’ monastery on the banks of the Serpentine river to see Sarabha the wanderer out of sympathy.”
translation_text.an3.64:3.1: Then in the late afternoon, the Buddha came out of retreat and went to the wanderers’ monastery on the banks of the Serpentine river to visit Sarabha the wanderer. He sat on the seat spread out, and said to the wanderer Sarabha,
translation_text.an3.64:3.2: “Is it really true, Sarabha, that you’ve been saying:
translation_text.an3.64:3.3: ‘I learned the teaching of the ascetics who follow the Sakyan,
translation_text.an3.64:3.4: then I left their teaching and training.’”
translation_text.an3.64:3.5: When he said this, Sarabha kept silent.
translation_text.an3.64:4.1: For a second time, the Buddha said to Sarabha,
translation_text.an3.64:4.2: “Tell me, Sarabha, what exactly have you learned of the teachings of the ascetics who follow the Sakyan?
translation_text.an3.64:4.3: If you’ve not learned it fully, I’ll fill you in.
translation_text.an3.64:4.4: But if you have learned it fully, I’ll agree.”
translation_text.an3.64:4.5: For a second time, Sarabha kept silent.
translation_text.an3.64:5.1: For a third time, the Buddha said to Sarabha,
translation_text.an3.64:5.2: “Sarabha, the teachings of the ascetics who follow the Sakyan are clear to me. What exactly have you learned of the teachings of the ascetics who follow the Sakyan?
translation_text.an3.64:5.3: If you’ve not learned it fully, I’ll fill you in.
translation_text.an3.64:5.4: But if you have learned it fully, I’ll agree.”
translation_text.an3.64:5.5: For a third time, Sarabha kept silent.
translation_text.an3.64:6.1: Then those wanderers said to Sarabha,
translation_text.an3.64:6.2: “The ascetic Gotama has offered to tell you anything you ask for.
translation_text.an3.64:6.3: Speak, reverend Sarabha, what exactly have you learned of the teachings of the ascetics who follow the Sakyan?
translation_text.an3.64:6.4: If you’ve not learned it fully, he’ll fill you in.
translation_text.an3.64:6.5: But if you have learned it fully, he’ll agree.”
translation_text.an3.64:6.6: When this was said, Sarabha sat silent, dismayed, shoulders drooping, downcast, depressed, with nothing to say.
translation_text.an3.64:7.1: Knowing this, the Buddha said to the wanderers:
translation_text.an3.64:8.2: ‘You claim to be a fully awakened Buddha, but you don’t understand these things.’ Then I’d carefully pursue, press, and grill them on that point.
translation_text.an3.64:8.3: When grilled by me, they would, without a doubt, fall into one of these three categories. They’d dodge the issue, distracting the discussion with irrelevant points. They’d display annoyance, hate, and bitterness. Or they’d sit silent, dismayed, shoulders drooping, downcast, depressed, with nothing to say, like Sarabha.
translation_text.an3.64:9.1: Wanderers, someone might say to me:
translation_text.an3.64:9.2: ‘You claim to have ended all defilements, but you still have these defilements.’ Then I’d carefully pursue, press, and grill them on that point.
translation_text.an3.64:9.3: When grilled by me, they would, without a doubt, fall into one of these three categories. They’d dodge the issue, distracting the discussion with irrelevant points. They’d display annoyance, hate, and bitterness. Or they’d sit silent, dismayed, shoulders drooping, downcast, depressed, with nothing to say, like Sarabha.
translation_text.an3.64:10.1: Wanderers, someone might say to me:
translation_text.an3.64:10.2: ‘Your teaching does not lead someone who practices it to the complete ending of suffering, the goal for which it is taught.’ Then I’d carefully pursue, press, and grill them on that point.
translation_text.an3.64:10.3: When grilled by me, they would, without a doubt, fall into one of these three categories. They’d dodge the issue, distracting the discussion with irrelevant points. They’d display annoyance, hate, and bitterness. Or they’d sit silent, dismayed, shoulders drooping, downcast, depressed, with nothing to say, like Sarabha.”
translation_text.an3.64:10.4: Then the Buddha, having roared his lion’s roar three times in the wanderers’ monastery on the bank of the Serpentine river, rose into the air and flew away.
translation_text.an3.64:11.1: Soon after the Buddha left, those wanderers beset Sarabha on all sides with sneering and jeering.
translation_text.an3.64:11.2: “Reverend Sarabha, you’re just like an old jackal in the formidable wilderness who thinks, ‘I’ll roar a lion’s roar!’ but they still only manage to squeal and yelp like a jackal.
translation_text.an3.64:11.3: In the same way, when the ascetic Gotama wasn’t here you said ‘I’ll roar a lion’s roar!’ but you only managed to squeal and yelp like a jackal.
translation_text.an3.64:11.4: You’re just like a marsh hen who thinks, ‘I’ll cry like a cuckoo!’ but they still only manage to cry like a marsh hen.
translation_text.an3.64:11.5: In the same way, when the ascetic Gotama wasn’t here you said ‘I’ll cry like a cuckoo!’ but you still only managed to cry like a marsh hen.
translation_text.an3.64:11.6: You’re just like a bull that thinks to bellow only when the cowstall is empty.
translation_text.an3.64:11.7: In the same way, you only thought to bellow when the ascetic Gotama wasn’t here.”
translation_text.an3.64:11.8: That’s how those wanderers beset Sarabha on all sides with sneering and jeering.

variant_text.an3.64:2.6: sippinikātīraṁ → sappinikātīraṁ (bj, pts1ed); sappiniyā tīraṁ (sya-all, km)
variant_text.an3.64:5.2: yo → etthantare pāṭho si potthake, mayā (sya-all, km, pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.64:8.3: nāññataraṁ → aññataraṁ (mr) | tuṇhībhūto maṅkubhūto → tuṇhībhūto vā maṅkubhūto (bj, sya-all, km); tuṇhībhūto vā maṅkubhūto vā (pts1ed)
variant_text.an3.64:11.2: siṅgālakaṁyeva → segālakaṁyeva (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed) | bheraṇḍakaṁyeva → bhedaṇḍakaṁ (mr)
variant_text.an3.64:11.4: ambukasañcarī → ambakamaddarī (bj, sya-all, pts1ed) | purisakaravitaṁ → phussakaravitaṁ (bj, pts1ed); pussakaravitaṁ (sya-all, km)
